title: Freesail Standard Catalog
description: Unified catalog of standard A2UI components and functions.

allOf.description: Renders text content. Key properties: text (required), variant (h1/h2/h3/h4/h5/caption/body), size (CSS font-size), weight (CSS font-weight), color (CSS color). User-Writable: false.

text.description: The text content to display. While simple Markdown formatting is supported (i.e. without HTML, images, or links), utilizing dedicated UI components is generally preferred for a richer and more structured presentation.

variant.description: A hint for the base text style.

size.description: CSS font-size for the text (e.g. '12px', '1.2rem', '2em'). Overrides the size implied by variant.

fontWeight.description: CSS font-weight (e.g. 'normal', 'bold', '500', '700'). Overrides the weight implied by variant.

color.description: CSS color for the text (e.g. '#ef4444', 'red', 'var(--freesail-primary)'). Defaults to inherited color.

allOf.description: Displays a Material Design icon. Key properties: name (required, a Material Icon name in camelCase). User-Writable: false.

name.description: A Material Icon name in camelCase (e.g. 'home', 'search', 'settings', 'accountCircle', 'shoppingCart'). Use any valid icon name from the Material Icons library.

color.description: CSS color for the icon (e.g. '#ef4444', 'red', 'var(--freesail-primary)'). Defaults to inherited color.

allOf.description: A layout component that arranges its children horizontally. To create a grid layout, nest Columns within this Row. Key properties: children (required), gap (spacing between children, default '8px'), padding, justify (start/center/end/spaceBetween/spaceAround/spaceEvenly/stretch), align (start/center/end/stretch; controls vertical alignment). User-Writable: false.

children.description: Defines the children. Use an array of strings for a fixed set of children, or a template object to generate children from a data list. Children cannot be defined inline, they must be referred to by ID.

justify.description: Defines the arrangement of children along the main axis (horizontally). Use 'spaceBetween' to push items to the edges, or 'start'/'end'/'center' to pack them together.

align.description: Defines the alignment of children along the cross axis (vertically). This is similar to the CSS 'align-items' property, but uses camelCase values (e.g., 'start').

wrap.description: Defines whether items should wrap to a new line if they exceed the container width.

allOf.description: A layout component that arranges its children vertically. To create a grid layout, nest Rows within this Column. Key properties: children (required), gap (spacing between children, default '8px'), padding, justify (start/center/end/spaceBetween/spaceAround/spaceEvenly/stretch), align (start/center/end/stretch; controls horizontal alignment of children; default is 'start'; set to 'stretch' for list layouts where children should fill the full width). User-Writable: false.

children.description: Defines the children. Use an array of strings for a fixed set of children, or a template object to generate children from a data list. Children cannot be defined inline, they must be referred to by ID.

justify.description: Defines the arrangement of children along the main axis (vertically). Use 'spaceBetween' to push items to the edges (e.g. header at top, footer at bottom), or 'start'/'end'/'center' to pack them together.

align.description: Defines the alignment of children along the cross axis (horizontally). This is similar to the CSS 'align-items' property.

allOf.description: A styled container with border and optional shadow. Two variants: 'raised' (default, elevated look with shadow and rounded corners) and 'flat' (no shadow, square corners, background matches the main surface). To make cards stretch to fill their parent's width, either set width to '100%' on the Card, or set align to 'stretch' on the parent Column. Key properties: child (required), variant ('raised'|'flat'), borderWeight (border thickness in px, default 1, 0 removes border), padding, width, height, background, zoomable. User-Writable: false.

variant.description: Visual style of the card. 'raised' (default) has a shadow and rounded corners on an elevated background. 'flat' has no shadow, square corners, and blends with the main surface background.

borderWeight.description: Border thickness in pixels. Default is 1. Set to 0 to remove the border entirely.

background.description: A CSS color value for the card's background fill. Accepts any valid CSS color, e.g. hex ('#22c55e'), named ('red'), rgb/rgba, hsl. Overrides the variant default background.

zoomable.description: When true, a small toggle button appears in the top-right corner of the card. Clicking it opens the card in a centered modal overlay at 70% of the viewport width (max 1200px, max 90vh tall); clicking outside or the button again restores it.

child.description: The ID of the single child component to be rendered inside the card. To display multiple elements, you MUST wrap them in a layout component (like Column or Row) and pass that container's ID here. Do NOT pass multiple IDs or a non-existent ID. Do NOT define the child component inline.

allOf.description: A modal window that overlays the screen. IMPORTANT: The Modal must be added to a parent layout's children array (e.g. the root Column) to render. It renders as a fixed overlay so its position in the tree does not affect layout. Visibility is controlled via the show/hide functions or the visible property (default false). To open the modal, use a Button whose action calls show with the modal's ID. Key properties: child (required, ID of the component shown inside the modal), visible (boolean, default false). User-Writable: false.

child.description: The ID of the component to be displayed inside the modal. Do NOT define the component inline.

allOf.description: A clickable button that triggers an action. Key properties: action (required), label (text for the button), child (ID of a child component, e.g. Icon), variant (primary/secondary/outline/borderless/danger; default is 'primary' which uses the theme's primary color). User-Writable: false.

label.description: The text label for the button. Used if 'child' is not provided.

child.description: The ID of the child component. Use a 'Text' component for a labeled button. Only use an 'Icon' if the requirements explicitly ask for an icon-only button. Do NOT define the child component inline.

variant.description: A hint for the button style. If omitted, a default button style is used.

allOf.description: A text input field for user input. Key properties: label (required), value (data binding for the input's value), variant (shortText/longText/number/obscured), hideLabel (set to true to suppress the label — use this when the TextField is inside a GridLayout where column headers already describe the field), min/max (numeric bounds, only used when variant is 'number'). User-Writable: true.

label.description: The text label for the input field.

value.description: The value of the text field.

variant.description: The type of input field to display.

hideLabel.description: When true, hides the label above the input. Use this when the TextField is inside a GridLayout — the column headers already act as labels, so showing them again inside each cell is redundant and makes rows too tall.

min.description: Minimum allowed value. Only used when variant is 'number'.

max.description: Maximum allowed value. Only used when variant is 'number'.

allOf.description: A component that allows selecting exactly one option from a list of options. User-Writable: true.

label.description: The label for the group of options.

variant.description: How the choice picker should be displayed - a group of radio buttons or chips.

options.description: The list of available options to choose from. Can be a literal array or a data binding to a list in the data model.

label.description: The text to display for this option.

value.description: The stable value associated with this option.
The currently selected value. This should be bound to a string in the data model.

allOf.description: A component that allows selecting one or more options from a list of options. User-Writable: true.

label.description: The label for the group of options.

variant.description: How the choice picker should be displayed - a group of checkboxes or chips.

options.description: The list of available options to choose from. Can be a literal array or a data binding to a list in the data model.

label.description: The text to display for this option.

value.description: The stable value associated with this option.
The list of currently selected values. This should be bound to a string array in the data model.

allOf.description: A date and/or time picker input. Key properties: value (required, ISO 8601 format), enableDate, enableTime, min, max, label. User-Writable: true.

value.description: The selected date and/or time value in ISO 8601 format. If not yet set, initialize with an empty string.

enableDate.description: If true, allows the user to select a date.

enableTime.description: If true, allows the user to select a time.

min.description: The minimum allowed date/time in ISO 8601 format.

max.description: The maximum allowed date/time in ISO 8601 format.

label.description: The text label for the input field.

allOf.description: An empty space component used for layout and spacing. User-Writable: false.
Displays an image. Key properties: url (required), fit (contain/cover/fill/none/scale-down), variant (icon/avatar/smallFeature/mediumFeature/largeFeature/header). User-Writable: false.

url.description: The URL of the image to display.

fit.description: Specifies how the image should be resized to fit its container. This corresponds to the CSS 'object-fit' property.

variant.description: A hint for the image size and style.

allOf.description: Plays a video. Key properties: url (required), embed (set to true for YouTube/Vimeo URLs). User-Writable: false.

url.description: The URL of the video to display.

embed.description: When true, the video is rendered inside an iframe. This MUST be set to true for third-party sources such as YouTube (youtube.com, youtu.be) and Vimeo (vimeo.com). When false or omitted, a native video player is used, which only works with direct video file URLs (e.g. .mp4, .webm).

allOf.description: An audio player that supports direct audio file URLs and embedded third-party players (e.g. Spotify, SoundCloud). User-Writable: false.

url.description: The URL of the audio to be played.

description.description: A description of the audio, such as a title or summary.

embed.description: When true, the audio is rendered inside an iframe embed player. This MUST be set to true for third-party sources such as Spotify (open.spotify.com) and SoundCloud (soundcloud.com). When false or omitted, a native audio player is used, which only works with direct audio file URLs (e.g. .mp3, .wav).

allOf.description: A scrollable list that lays out children vertically or horizontally, with optional alignment control. User-Writable: false.

children.description: Defines the children. Use an array of strings for a fixed set of children, or a template object to generate children from a data list.

direction.description: The direction in which the list items are laid out.

align.description: Defines the alignment of children along the cross axis.

allOf.description: A tabbed container that shows one Tab child at a time. Children must be Tab components. User-Writable: false.

children.description: Array of Tab component IDs. Do NOT define components inline.

allOf.description: A single tab within a TabGroup. Has a title and a child component. User-Writable: false.

title.description: The tab title displayed in the tab bar.

child.description: The ID of the child component. Do NOT define the component inline.

allOf.description: A visual separator line. Key properties: axis (horizontal/vertical, default horizontal). User-Writable: false.
A dropdown (select) component for choosing a single option from a list. User-Writable: true.

hideLabel.description: When true, hides the label above the dropdown. Use this when the Dropdown is inside a GridLayout — the column headers already act as labels.

placeholder.description: Placeholder text shown when no option is selected.

options.description: The list of available options to choose from. Can be a literal array or a data binding to a list in the data model.

label.description: The text to display for this option.

value.description: The stable value associated with this option.
The currently selected value. This should be bound to a string in the data model.

allOf.description: A checkbox toggle for boolean values. Key properties: label (required), value (required, data binding for the checked state). User-Writable: true.

label.description: The text to display next to the checkbox.

value.description: The current state of the checkbox (true for checked, false for unchecked).

allOf.description: A slider for selecting a numeric value within a range. Key properties: value (required), min (required), max (required), label. User-Writable: true.

min.description: The minimum value of the slider.

max.description: The maximum value of the slider.

value.description: The current value of the slider.

allOf.description: A flexible grid component with two modes. TABULAR MODE (when headers is provided): displays data with a header row, styled alternating rows, and borders — collapses to a single column on narrow containers. Compose row content using Row and Text components via the children template. FLUID MODE (when headers is omitted): an auto-fill responsive grid that flows children into equal-width columns of at least minItemWidth each. Key properties: headers (optional, array of column header strings — determines tabular vs fluid mode), children (required, ChildList template or array of component IDs), rowPadding (tabular mode only, controls cell padding; default '10px 16px'), minItemWidth (fluid mode only, minimum column width; default '200px'), gap (fluid mode only, gap between items; default uses --freesail-space-sm). IMPORTANT: Row padding is controlled by rowPadding on the Grid, not by padding on the child Row components. IMPORTANT: When placing TextField or Dropdown components inside a Grid table, always set hideLabel: true on each one. User-Writable: false.

headers.description: Column header labels. When provided, the component renders in tabular mode with a header row. When omitted, renders in fluid auto-fill mode.

children.description: Defines the row/item content. Use a template object { componentId, path } to generate items from a data list, or an array of component IDs for static children.

rowPadding.description: (Tabular mode) CSS padding applied to every cell in each data row. Defaults to '10px 16px'.

minItemWidth.description: (Fluid mode) Minimum width of each auto-fill column. Defaults to '200px'. Use CSS length values e.g. '160px', '12rem'.

gap.description: (Fluid mode) Gap between grid items. Defaults to var(--freesail-space-sm). Use CSS length or spacing token.

allOf.description: Renders a bar chart from an array of data points. Each data point has a label and value. The chart auto-scales to fit. Use orientation 'horizontal' for long labels or many categories. User-Writable: false.

title.description: Optional chart title displayed above the chart.

data.description: Array of data points. Each item has a 'label' (string) and 'value' (number), and optionally a 'color' (CSS color string) to override the default bar color for that item.

orientation.description: Bar direction. 'vertical' = bars grow upward, 'horizontal' = bars grow rightward.

color.description: Default bar color (CSS). Individual data items can override with their own 'color'.

showValues.description: Whether to display the numeric value on each bar.

allOf.description: Renders a line chart from an array of data points. Supports optional area fill and data point dots. Good for showing trends over time. User-Writable: false.

title.description: Optional chart title displayed above the chart.

data.description: Array of data points. Each item has a 'label' (string) and 'value' (number).

showDots.description: Whether to show data point circles on the line.

showArea.description: Whether to fill the area under the line with a translucent gradient.

allOf.description: Renders a pie or donut chart from an array of segments. Each segment has a label and value. Colors are auto-assigned if not provided. User-Writable: false.

title.description: Optional chart title displayed above the chart.

data.description: Array of segments. Each item has a 'label' (string) and 'value' (number), and optionally a 'color' (CSS color string).

donut.description: If true, renders as a donut chart with a hollow center.

allOf.description: Renders a compact inline sparkline chart from an array of numbers. Use inside StatCard or alongside text to show a quick trend. User-Writable: false.

values.description: Array of numeric values representing the trend data.

allOf.description: Renders a KPI / summary statistic card with a large value, label, and optional trend indicator. Use multiple StatCards in a Row to create a dashboard header. User-Writable: false.

value.description: The formatted display value (e.g. '$12,340', '1.2k', '98%'). Use formatNumber or formatCurrency to produce this.

trendValue.description: The trend change text shown next to the arrow (e.g. '+12%', '-3.2%').

color.description: Accent color for the card's left border and icon.

children.description: Optional children rendered below the stat value (e.g. a Sparkline).

required.description: Checks that the value is not null, undefined, or empty.

regex.description: Checks that the value matches a regular expression string.

pattern.description: The regex pattern to match against.

checkLength.description: Returns true if the string or array length satisfies the given constraints. Takes two arguments: the value and an object with optional 'min' and/or 'max' integer properties. Example: checkLength(value, {min: 8}) returns true if value has at least 8 characters. Always returns a boolean. To get the length as a number, use getLength instead.

getLength.description: Returns the number of characters in a value. If the value is a string, returns its length. If the value is a number or date, converts it to a string first and returns the length of that string. If the value is an array, returns the number of elements. Returns 0 for null or undefined. Use this when you need the length as a number (e.g. for comparisons with gte). Use checkLength instead if you just need to validate min/max constraints.

numeric.description: Returns true if the numeric value satisfies the given constraints. Takes two arguments: the value and an object with optional 'min' and/or 'max' number properties. Example: numeric(value, {min: 0, max: 100}) returns true if value is between 0 and 100. Always returns a boolean.

email.description: Checks that the value is a valid email address.

formatString.description: Performs string interpolation of data model values and other functions in the catalog functions list and returns the resulting string. The value string can contain interpolated expressions in the `${expression}` format. Supported expression types include: JSON Pointer paths to the data model (e.g., `${/absolute/path}` or `${relative/path}`), and client-side function calls (e.g., `${now()}`). Function arguments must be literals (quoted strings, numbers, booleans) or nested expressions (e.g., `${formatDate(${/currentDate}, 'MM-dd')}`). To include a literal `${` sequence, escape it as `\${`.

formatNumber.description: Formats a number with the specified grouping and decimal precision.

fractionDigits.description: Optional. The number of decimal places to show. Defaults to 0 or 2 depending on locale.

useGrouping.description: Optional. If true, uses locale-specific grouping separators (e.g. '1,000'). If false, returns raw digits (e.g. '1000'). Defaults to true.

formatCurrency.description: Formats a number as a currency string.

formatDate.description: Formats a timestamp into a string using a pattern.

pattern.description:
A Unicode TR35 date pattern string.

Token Reference:
- Year: 'yy' (26), 'yyyy' (2026)
- Month: 'M' (1), 'MM' (01), 'MMM' (Jan), 'MMMM' (January)
- Day: 'd' (1), 'dd' (01), 'E' (Tue), 'EEEE' (Tuesday)
- Hour (12h): 'h' (1-12), 'hh' (01-12) - requires 'a' for AM/PM
- Hour (24h): 'H' (0-23), 'HH' (00-23) - Military Time
- Minute: 'mm' (00-59)
- Second: 'ss' (00-59)
- Period: 'a' (AM/PM)

Examples:
- 'MMM dd, yyyy' -> 'Jan 16, 2026'
- 'HH:mm' -> '14:30' (Military)
- 'h:mm a' -> '2:30 PM'
- 'EEEE, d MMMM' -> 'Friday, 16 January'

pluralize.description: Returns a localized string based on the Common Locale Data Repository (CLDR) plural category of the count (zero, one, two, few, many, other). Requires an 'other' fallback. For English, just use 'one' and 'other'.

count.description: The numeric value used to determine the plural category.

forms.description: A map of CLDR plural categories to their corresponding strings.

two.description: String for the 'two' category (used in Arabic, Welsh, etc.).

few.description: String for the 'few' category (e.g., small groups in Slavic languages).

many.description: String for the 'many' category (e.g., large groups in various languages).

other.description: The default/fallback string (used for general plural cases).

now.description: Returns the current date and time as an ISO 8601 string.

openUrl.description: Opens the specified URL in a browser or handler. This function has no return value.

and.description: Returns true if all arguments are truthy.

or.description: Returns true if any argument is truthy.

isEmpty.description: Returns true if the value is null, undefined, empty string, empty array, or empty object.

eq.description: Returns true if the two values are strictly equal.

neq.description: Returns true if the two values are not equal.

gt.description: Returns true if the first value is greater than the second (converted to numbers).

gte.description: Returns true if the first value is greater than or equal to the second (converted to numbers).

lt.description: Returns true if the first value is less than the second (converted to numbers).

lte.description: Returns true if the first value is less than or equal to the second (converted to numbers).

show.description: Shows a component by its ID. Use this to make Modals or any other component visible.

componentId.description: The ID of the component to show.

hide.description: Hides a component by its ID. Use this to hide Modals or any other component.

componentId.description: The ID of the component to hide.

component.description: REQUIRED. The component type name (e.g. 'Text', 'Button', 'Column'). Each component's schema further constrains this to its exact const value.

visible.description: Controls whether the component is rendered. Defaults to true. Use a function call to compute dynamically (e.g. show return date only when trip type is round-trip).

weight.description: The relative weight of this component within a Row or Column. This is similar to the CSS 'flex-grow' property. Note: this may ONLY be set when the component is a direct descendant of a Row or Column.

flexBasis.description: The flex-basis of this component within a Row or Column. Defines the default size of an element before remaining space is distributed. Essential for fluid responsive breakpoints (e.g., '300px').

ComponentId.description: The unique identifier for a component, used for both definitions and references within the same surface.

AccessibilityAttributes.description: Attributes to enhance accessibility when using assistive technologies like screen readers.

label.description: A short string, typically 1 to 3 words, used by assistive technologies to convey the purpose or intent of an element. For example, an input field might have an accessible label of 'User ID' or a button might be labeled 'Submit'.

description.description: Additional information provided by assistive technologies about an element such as instructions, format requirements, or result of an action. For example, a mute button might have a label of 'Mute' and a description of 'Silences notifications about this conversation'.

path.description: A JSON Pointer path to a value in the data model.

FunctionCall.description: Invokes a named function on the client.

call.description: The name of the function to call.

returnType.description: The expected return type of the function call.

DynamicValue.description: A value that can be a literal, a path, or a function call returning any type.

DynamicBoolean.description: A boolean value that can be a literal, a path, or a function call returning a boolean.

oneOf.description: A static list of child component IDs.
A template for generating a dynamic list of children from a data model list. The `componentId` is the component to use as a template.

path.description: The path to the list of component property objects in the data model.

Checkable.description: Properties for components that support client-side checks.

checks.description: A list of checks to perform. These are function calls that must return a boolean indicating validity.

CheckRule.description: A single validation rule applied to an input component.

message.description: The error message to display if the check fails.

Action.description: Defines an interaction handler that can either trigger a server-side event or execute a local client-side function.

event.description: The event to dispatch to the server.

name.description: The name of the action to be dispatched to the server.

context.description: A JSON object containing the key-value pairs for the action context. Values can be literals or paths. Use literal values unless the value must be dynamically bound to the data model. Do NOT use paths for static IDs.

DynamicNumber.description: Represents a value that can be either a literal number, a path to a number in the data model, or a function call returning a number.

DynamicStringList.description: Represents a value that can be either a literal array of strings, a path to a string array in the data model, or a function call returning a string array.
